metadata.summary: sysctl parameter tuning for Ubuntu 24.04 VPS: network performance (BBR + TCP), agent tuning (inotify), memory management, and security hardening via drop-in files.

content.title: Kernel Tuning
content.summary: sysctl parameter tuning for Ubuntu 24.04 VPS running web services and AI agent workloads. Four drop-in files under `/etc/sysctl.d/`: network performance (BBR + TCP buffers), agent tuning (inotify watches), memory management (swappiness), and security hardening. Apply with `sudo sysctl --system`. The critical non-obvious rule: inotify `max_user_watches` defaults to 65536 — Claude Code exhausts this on large codebases, causing "No space left on device" errors on file watchers.

why.p: Default kernel parameters are conservative and general-purpose. A VPS running AI agents needs 8x the default inotify watches (524288 vs 65536); a web server benefits from BBR congestion control (~18% throughput gain); a server with abundant RAM should set swappiness=10 to avoid unnecessary swap thrash. Files under `/etc/sysctl.d/` are processed in alphabetical order — use prefix `60-` to override distro defaults without modifying their files.

list.item: New server setup: apply all four sysctl.d files as standard baseline
Claude Code or file watchers emit "ENOSPC: inotify watches reached" — increase `fs.inotify.max_user_watches`
Server swaps aggressively despite available RAM — set `vm.swappiness=10`
WebSocket/API performance is below expectations — enable TCP BBR
Hardening a production VPS against information leakage
Kubernetes nodes — resource limits are managed at the pod/container level, not via host sysctl
Shared hosting where root access is not available
Containers (Docker) — sysctl changes in containers require `--sysctl` flag or privileged mode

rule.statement: Place custom parameters in `/etc/sysctl.d/60-*.conf`. Files are loaded alphabetically — prefix `60-` ensures overriding Ubuntu's `10-*` defaults without editing them.
rule.rationale: Editing distro-provided files under `10-*` causes confusing diffs during OS upgrades. The `60-` prefix gives a clean namespace above distro defaults but below emergency overrides at `99-`.

example.code:
# Apply all sysctl configs (including new drop-ins) sudo sysctl --system
# Apply a single file sudo sysctl -p /etc/sysctl.d/60-agent-tuning.conf
# Verify a value sysctl fs.inotify.max_user_watches

section.name: TCP BBR and Network Performance

rule.statement: Enable BBR congestion control with `fq` qdisc. BBR outperforms the default Cubic on high-latency connections, giving ~18% throughput improvement on transatlantic and cloud-to-cloud traffic.
rule.rationale: BBR models network bandwidth directly instead of reacting to packet loss. Combined with the `fq` (Fair Queue) qdisc (not `fq_codel`), it paces traffic accurately and reduces latency for WebSocket and long-lived API connections.

example.code:
# /etc/sysctl.d/60-network-perf.conf net.core.default_qdisc = fq net.ipv4.tcp_congestion_control = bbr
# TCP buffer sizes (for 30GB RAM server) net.core.rmem_max = 16777216 net.core.wmem_max = 16777216 net.ipv4.tcp_rmem = 4096 87380 16777216 net.ipv4.tcp_wmem = 4096 65536 16777216
# Connection handling net.core.somaxconn = 65535 net.core.netdev_max_backlog = 5000 net.ipv4.tcp_max_syn_backlog = 8192 net.ipv4.tcp_fin_timeout = 30 net.ipv4.tcp_keepalive_time = 300 net.ipv4.tcp_keepalive_intvl = 60 net.ipv4.tcp_keepalive_probes = 5

section.note: Scale `rmem_max`/`wmem_max` with available RAM. For 4GB servers use 4194304; for 8GB use 8388608.

rule.statement: Set `fs.inotify.max_user_watches = 524288`. The default (65536) is exhausted by Claude Code on large codebases, triggering "ENOSPC: System limit for number of file watchers reached" errors.
rule.rationale: Claude Code creates one inotify watch per tracked file. A 50k-file repository consumes 50k watches. With multiple worktrees or VS Code also running, the default 65536 ceiling is hit immediately. 524288 (512k) supports ~5 concurrent large workspaces.

example.code: # Diagnose inotify usage: which processes consume the most watches find /proc/*/fd -lname anon_inode:inotify 2>/dev/null \ | cut -d/ -f3 \ | xargs -I{} sh -c 'cat /proc/{}/cmdline 2>/dev/null | tr "\0" " "; echo' \ | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -10

rule.statement: Set `vm.swappiness = 10` on servers with 8GB+ RAM running interactive workloads. Never set it to 0 — that risks OOM kills when RAM fills suddenly.
rule.rationale: Default swappiness=60 causes aggressive paging even when many gigabytes of RAM are free, adding latency to LLM API calls. Value 10 means: start swapping when only ~10% of RAM remains, keeping hot data in RAM while still having a swap safety net.

rule.statement: Apply four security parameters as a baseline: hide kernel addresses (`kptr_restrict=1`), restrict ptrace (`ptrace_scope=1`), restrict dmesg (`dmesg_restrict=1`), enable SYN cookies (`tcp_syncookies=1`).
rule.rationale: These four parameters close the most common information-leakage vectors without affecting normal application behavior. `kptr_restrict` prevents KASLR bypass; `ptrace_scope=1` limits process inspection to parent/child; SYN cookies protect against SYN flood DDoS.

example.code:
# /etc/sysctl.d/60-security.conf kernel.kptr_restrict = 1 kernel.yama.ptrace_scope = 1 kernel.dmesg_restrict = 1 kernel.randomize_va_space = 2 kernel.sysrq = 176
net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies = 1 net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0 net.ipv4.conf.all.send_redirects = 0 net.ipv4.conf.all.rp_filter = 2 net.ipv4.conf.all.log_martians = 1 net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0

rule.statement: Set per-user `nofile` to 65535 in `/etc/security/limits.d/`. The kernel `fs.file-max` rarely needs changing; per-user PAM limits are the practical bottleneck.
rule.rationale: RabbitMQ, Node.js, and Python asyncio services open many file descriptors. The default soft limit of 1024 is hit almost immediately by production services. PAM limits propagate to all processes started in user sessions, including systemd user services.

example.code: # /etc/security/limits.d/99-nero.conf nero soft nofile 65535 nero hard nofile 65535 nero soft nproc 65535 nero hard nproc 65535

section.name: Diagnosing inotify Exhaustion
section.p: Symptom: Claude Code or VS Code shows "ENOSPC: System limit for number of file watchers reached" even though disk is not full.

example.code:
# Check current limit vs usage sysctl fs.inotify.max_user_watches # fs.inotify.max_user_watches = 65536 ← too low
# Count watches consumed by each process find /proc/*/fd -lname anon_inode:inotify 2>/dev/null \ | cut -d/ -f3 \ | xargs -I{} sh -c 'cat /proc/{}/cmdline 2>/dev/null | tr "\0" " "; echo' \ | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -10
# Apply fix immediately (survives until next reboot) echo fs.inotify.max_user_watches=524288 | sudo tee /etc/sysctl.d/60-agent-tuning.conf sudo sysctl --system
# Verify sysctl fs.inotify.max_user_watches # fs.inotify.max_user_watches = 524288 ← fixed

# Check congestion control sysctl net.ipv4.tcp_congestion_control # net.ipv4.tcp_congestion_control = bbr
# Check qdisc tc qdisc show dev eth0 # qdisc fq 8001: root refcnt 2 ...
# If still showing cubic after applying conf: sudo sysctl --system sysctl net.ipv4.tcp_congestion_control # Should now show bbr

# Before: aggressive swapping at 60 sysctl vm.swappiness # vm.swappiness = 60
# Apply fix echo "vm.swappiness = 10" | sudo tee /etc/sysctl.d/60-memory.conf sudo sysctl vm.swappiness=10
# Verify sysctl vm.swappiness # vm.swappiness = 10
# Check current swap usage free -h # Swap: 4.0Gi 0B 4.0Gi ← no active swap with RAM available

# Check all four security baseline values sysctl kernel.kptr_restrict kernel.yama.ptrace_scope kernel.dmesg_restrict net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies # kernel.kptr_restrict = 1 # kernel.yama.ptrace_scope = 1 # kernel.dmesg_restrict = 1 # net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies = 1

# System-level file descriptor usage cat /proc/sys/fs/file-nr # 12480 0 2097152 # ↑ allocated ↑ free from allocated ↑ system max
# Per-process FD count (find high consumers) for pid in /proc/[0-9]*; do count=$(ls "$pid/fd" 2>/dev/null | wc -l) name=$(cat "$pid/comm" 2>/dev/null) echo "$count $name" done | sort -rn | head -10
# Verify PAM limits applied (must be in a new login session) ulimit -n # 65535

section.name: Connection Tracking for WebSocket Services
section.p: nginx WebSocket proxy and RabbitMQ AMQP connections consume conntrack table entries. Monitor usage when serving many concurrent long-lived connections.

example.code:
# Check conntrack usage vs limit sudo conntrack -C 2>/dev/null || cat /proc/sys/net/netfilter/nf_conntrack_count # 8241
sysctl net.netfilter.nf_conntrack_max # net.netfilter.nf_conntrack_max = 262144
# If count/max ratio > 80%, increase nf_conntrack_max echo "net.netfilter.nf_conntrack_max = 524288" | sudo tee -a /etc/sysctl.d/60-network-perf.conf sudo sysctl net.netfilter.nf_conntrack_max=524288

template.purpose: TCP BBR, buffer sizes, connection handling, keepalive
inotify watches, file descriptors, process limits
Kernel address leakage, PTRACE, ASLR, ICMP hardening
Audit current values vs recommended, print diff table
